initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Maximum row and column indices (for the outer border)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border cells  explicit predicates for each side
border_left(R,C)  :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C):- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Cells that start the outside region  any border cell that is not a colour3 wall
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C),   input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C),  input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% 4connected neighbourhood (constant offsets  safe arithmetic)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C)   :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Propagation of the outside region through nonwall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells  nonwall cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints  exactly one colour is allowed for each cell
% Wall cells (colour3) stay colour3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zero cells become colour2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior nonzero cells (including colour2) keep their original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zero cells stay colour0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% Outside nonzero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% The chosen colour must be the allowed colour for the cell
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set  choose the allowed colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Maximum row and column indices (for the outer border)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border cells  explicit predicates for each side
border_left(R,C)  :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C):- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Cells that start the outside region  any border cell that is not a colour3 wall
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C),   input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C),  input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% 4connected neighbourhood (constant offsets  safe arithmetic)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C)   :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Propagation of the outside region through nonwall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells  nonwall cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints  exactly one colour is allowed for each cell
% Wall cells (colour3) stay colour3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zero cells become colour2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior nonzero cells (including colour2) keep their original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zero cells stay colour0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% Outside nonzero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% The chosen colour must be the allowed colour for the cell
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set  choose the allowed colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).

final_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Maximum row and column indices (for the outer border)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border cells  explicit predicates for each side
border_left(R,C)  :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C):- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Cells that start the outside region  any border cell that is not a colour3 wall
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C),   input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C),  input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% 4connected neighbourhood (constant offsets  safe arithmetic)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C)   :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Propagation of the outside region through nonwall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells  nonwall cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints  exactly one colour is allowed for each cell
% Wall cells (colour3) stay colour3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zero cells become colour2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior nonzero cells (including colour2) keep their original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zero cells stay colour0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% Outside nonzero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% The chosen colour must be the allowed colour for the cell
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set  choose the allowed colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
